title: Correction artifact (mechanism 4 — correction intake)
description: Append-only record of a human correction signal ('this is not pixel perfect!') or a deterministic auto-detected correction event (waiver created; UAT bug filed against a gate that rendered PASS). Written by scripts/correct.mjs to retro/corrections/<seq>-<slug>.correction.json. NEVER edited in place except to set `disposition` (null -> object). Undispositioned corrections render as red OPEN-CORRECTION lines that no gate may pass over.

id.description: Sequential id, COR-<n>, matching the numeric prefix of the file name.

ts.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of intake (not of the underlying event).

utterance.description: The verbatim human correction, or an AUTO: sentence generated by a detector.

contradictedClaim.description: file:line of the claim the correction contradicts (e.g. docs/current-state.md:85), or null when unknown.

mappedReqIds.description: Requirement ids the correction maps to. Empty array when not yet mapped.

gateThatShouldHaveCaught.description: The gate that rendered PASS but should have gone red, or null when unknown.

failureMode.description: How the process failed. Heuristic default is wrong-claim; overridable at intake via --failure-mode.

failureModeSource.description: Whether failureMode was heuristically inferred, explicitly passed, or set by an auto-detector.

source.description: human = CLI intake; auto-* = deterministic detector (waiver creation / UAT bug filed against a gate id).

autoKey.description: Detector dedupe key '<type>:<relPath>' for auto corrections; null for human intake.

disposition.description: ALWAYS null at intake. Set exactly once when the correction is root-caused: this is the only permitted mutation of a correction artifact.

status.description: resolved = fixed with a Refs: PD-x commit; waived = human-signed acceptance; invalid = correction was mistaken (say why in note).

by.description: Who dispositioned (human name/handle — never an agent).

note.description: Root cause / PD-x link / waiver rationale.
